2603.19635.title: BEAVER: A Training-Free Hierarchical Prompt Compression Method via Structure-Aware Page Selection
2603.19635.paper_summary: The exponential expansion of context windows in LLMs has unlocked capabilities for long-document understanding but introduced severe bottlenecks in inference latency and information utilization. Existing compression methods often suffer from high training costs or semantic fragmentation due to aggressive token pruning. In this paper, we propose BEAVER, a novel training-free framework that shifts compression from linear token removal to structure-aware hierarchical selection. BEAVER maximizes hardware parallelism by mapping variable-length contexts into dense page-level tensors via dual-path pooling, and preserves discourse integrity through a hybrid planner combining semantic and lexical dual-branch selection with sentence smoothing. Extensive evaluations on four long-context benchmarks demonstrate that BEAVER achieves comparable performance to state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods like LongLLMLingua. Notably, on the RULER benchmark, BEAVER maintains high fidelity in multi-needle retrieval where baselines deteriorate. Regarding efficiency, BEAVER reduces latency by 26.4x on 128k contexts, offering a scalable solution for high-throughput applications. Our code is available at https://cslikai.cn/BEAVER/.
2603.19635.paper_summary_zh: 上下文窗口在大型語言模型（LLMs）中的指數擴展解鎖了長文檔理解的能力，但也引入了推理延遲和信息利用方面的嚴重瓶頸。現有的壓縮方法通常因為激進的標記修剪而面臨高訓練成本或語義碎片化的問題。在本文中，我們提出了BEAVER，一種新穎的無需訓練的框架，將壓縮從線性標記移除轉變為結構感知的分層選擇。BEAVER通過雙路徑池化將變長的上下文映射到密集的頁面級張量，最大化硬件並行性，並通過結合語義和詞彙雙分支選擇與句子平滑的混合規劃者來保持話語完整性。在四個長上下文基準上的廣泛評估顯示，BEAVER達到了與最先進（SOTA）方法如LongLLMLingua相媲美的性能。值得注意的是，在RULER基準上，BEAVER在多針檢索中保持了高保真度，而基線則出現了惡化。關於效率，BEAVER在128k上下文中將延遲降低了26.4倍，為高吞吐量應用提供了可擴展的解決方案。我們的代碼可在https://cslikai.cn/BEAVER/獲得。
2603.19635.author: Zhengpei Hu et.al.
2603.19635.authors: Zhengpei Hu, Kai Li, Dapeng Fu, Chang Zeng, Yue Li, Yuanhao Tang, Jianqiang Huang
